summary: Install-time host recon exfiltrated to remote n8n endpoint with hardcoded JWT
details: On npm install, preinstall.js unconditionally executes `id && hostname && whoami && uname -a && cat /etc/os-release` via child_process.execSync and POSTs the captured host-identity output to https://worker.n8n-prod.schibsted.com/rest/variables. The request authenticates with a hardcoded n8n session JWT (`n8n-auth=eyJ...`) embedded in the script, and sets `rejectUnauthorized: false`, disabling TLS certificate validation for the install-time egress channel. The script additionally writes proof artifacts to /tmp and POSTs to the local n8n API at 127.0.0.1:5678. Installer harm: every machine that runs `npm install` ships its host identity off-host to a third-party domain over a MITM-vulnerable channel; the bundled JWT is also redistributed to all installers, who then carry credentials authorized to write into a third-party n8n instance.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
